name: High alert concentration (volume-adjusted)
description: Entity's alert rate is statistically elevated above the 16.0% portfolio base rate after requiring a minimum sample size
calculation_logic: binomial test vs base rate, n>=15, one-sided p<0.01; capped contribution regardless of how far above threshold
why_it_matters: Existing monitoring system has independently and repeatedly flagged this entity's activity
innocent_explanation: Legitimate complex trade pattern that happens to trip routine monitoring rules more often (e.g. higher-risk corridor mix by chance, larger transaction sizes)
false_positive_risk: Medium - alerts are themselves imperfect detectors with their own false-positive rate; concentration of alerts is not concentration of confirmed issues
weight_proposal: 3 points (capped) if p<0.01 and n>=15; 1 point if p<0.05 and n>=10
validation_check: Reproduced independently in SQL (Stage 4) and Python (Stage 5); stable under product-mix, corridor-mix, segment, and time-period controls

name: Exclusive customer-counterparty cluster relationship
description: A customer conducts an unusually large share (>=80%) of its transaction volume/value with a small, entity-resolution-linked counterparty cluster (n<=5 members)
calculation_logic: Share of customer's txn count AND value with cluster members >= 80%
why_it_matters: Concentration risk is elevated when nearly all business runs through a small, potentially-related counterparty set rather than a diversified customer base
innocent_explanation: Exclusive distributor/agency/franchise agreement; single-supplier or single-buyer relationship is a normal commercial structure, especially for SME exporters
false_positive_risk: Medium - exclusivity alone is common in legitimate small-business trade relationships
validation_check: Network view (Stage 6) cross-checked against entity-resolution cluster membership; re-verified transaction-level exclusivity independently

name: Shared bank account (any)
description: Two or more counterparties share an identical bank_account_hash value
calculation_logic: Exact match, pairwise, self-join with id_a<id_b
why_it_matters: Shared banking detail is one of the stronger entity-resolution signals - harder to collide coincidentally than an address string
innocent_explanation: Shared corporate treasury/holding company banking arrangement across legitimately separate subsidiaries
validation_check: Cross-checked against email/phone match (Stage 6) to rule out simple duplicate-data-entry explanation before scoring

name: Shared bank account PLUS an independent attribute
description: A shared-bank-account pair also shares registered_address, a declared relationship, or a near-duplicate name - i.e. Stage 6 'STRONG' classification
calculation_logic: Stage 6 classification = STRONG AND does not also match on email/phone (i.e. excludes CL003-type duplicate signature)
why_it_matters: Convergence of two independent identifiers substantially reduces the coincidence explanation versus either alone
innocent_explanation: Same corporate family using shared registered-agent services and shared group banking
weight_proposal: +1 bonus point on top of IND03 (i.e. STRONG pairs score 3 total, capped)
validation_check: Explicit exclusion of email/phone-matching pairs (duplicate signature) from this indicator, per Stage 6 finding

name: Declared relationship overlap (standalone)
description: A counterparty pair has a declared Entity_Relationships edge but no shared bank account
why_it_matters: A documented corporate link, even without shared banking, is relevant context for network read-across
innocent_explanation: Ordinary declared group structure with no bearing on transaction risk
false_positive_risk: Low (it's a documented fact) but LOW RELEVANCE risk is high - a declared link by itself says little about transaction risk
validation_check: Distinguish source='regulatory_filing' (more reliable) from source='declared' (self-reported, lower reliability) - both scored the same here pending better provenance data

name: Value/weight implausibility
description: Invoice value per declared shipment kg is a large multiple of the product category's average
calculation_logic: ratio-vs-category-mean, threshold sensitivity-tested at 3x/5x/10x/20x/50x (Stage 5); framework uses the 3-method-STABLE set (ratio AND robust-z AND IQR agree) as the qualifying condition, not a single fixed cut
why_it_matters: May indicate mis-invoicing, mis-declared goods, or valuation manipulation
innocent_explanation: Legitimate high-value/low-weight goods (electronics, pharma actives), unit/currency entry variance, premium product variant
false_positive_risk: Medium-High - Stage 5 showed candidate count ranges 22 to 157 depending on method/threshold
weight_proposal: 2 points (capped) - only for the 85-transaction STABLE set common to all 3 detection methods
validation_check: Threshold sensitivity table retained and shown alongside any flagged transaction - never presented as a single fixed number

name: Third-party payer route
description: Final settlement leg paid by an entity other than the customer
why_it_matters: Occasionally associated with layering in genuine financial-crime typologies
innocent_explanation: Factoring, group treasury consolidation, assigned receivables - all standard trade-finance practice
false_positive_risk: High - Stage 5 found the apparent 100% alert co-occurrence for this indicator is CIRCULAR (alert_type restates the same fact) and must not be double-counted
weight_proposal: 1 point MAXIMUM - explicitly low/moderate per instruction, and must not be combined with any alert-based bonus for the same transaction
validation_check: Confirmed circularity in Stage 5; framework scoring code explicitly excludes IND01 alert bonus from stacking with IND07 for the same transaction

name: Claim with prior alert
description: A claim's underlying transaction had >=1 alert on record before the claim
calculation_logic: EXISTS alert for the same transaction_id
evidence_category: 3 - Descriptive relationship (strong at portfolio level, not causal)
why_it_matters: Combines two independent monitoring/outcome signals on the same transaction
innocent_explanation: Both alerts and claims may independently reflect the same underlying transaction complexity or counterparty risk profile, without one causing the other
false_positive_risk: Medium - portfolio-level relationship is robust (Stage 5, chi2 p=1.2e-18) but any single instance could be coincidental
validation_check: Portfolio relationship re-verified in Stage 5 across segment and value-band strata

name: Date/data-quality issue exposure
description: Entity's transactions are disproportionately affected by logged Stage 3 data-quality issues (date-logic errors, missing values)
calculation_logic: Share of entity's transactions affected by a DQ0001/DQ0002-type issue vs portfolio rate
evidence_category: 6 - Intelligence gap (NOT an aggravating risk signal)
why_it_matters: Reduces confidence in any OTHER indicator computed on the same records - a caveat, not evidence of anything
innocent_explanation: Routine data entry error, system lag, incomplete shipment record
false_positive_risk: N/A - this indicator only ever REDUCES confidence, never increases priority
weight_proposal: 0 points; applied as a CONFIDENCE DISCOUNT modifier only
validation_check: Cross-referenced against Stage 3 issue register by table/record
allowed_in_framework: YES, as a discount modifier only - never a positive score contributor

name: Intermediary hub concentration
description: An intermediary handles a disproportionate share of portfolio transaction volume
calculation_logic: Share of distinct linked transactions handled by one intermediary
evidence_category: 3 - Descriptive relationship (concentration is a fact; risk is not)
why_it_matters: COULD indicate a chokepoint entity of interest - but ONLY if combined with independent indicators on the SAME intermediary
innocent_explanation: Ordinary large-scale freight-forwarder/correspondent-bank serving a broad, undifferentiated client base (this is what Stage 6 found for INTM00038)
false_positive_risk: Very high if scored on centrality alone - explicitly the trap this framework must not fall into
weight_proposal: 0 points from concentration alone. Only scores if the SAME intermediary independently shows an elevated alert/claim rate vs baseline (which INTM00038 does not: 16.01%/3.04% vs 16.0%/3.0% baseline)
validation_check: Stage 6 explicitly tested INTM00038's alert/claim rate against baseline and found no elevation - EXCLUDED from scoring on this basis
allowed_in_framework: YES, but gated - contributes 0 points unless a companion condition (elevated own alert/claim rate) is independently met
